title: Audit PPO/eval/DDP correctness and add regression tests

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Starting correctness audit of PPO, eval, DDP, seeding, and train/eval consistency before further experiments.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented correctness fixes for SAME_STEP vector env handling, truncation-aware bootstrap, deterministic main-rank eval, weighted episode metrics, checkpoint resume, and added regression tests. pytest is green.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed PPO/eval/DDP audit and fixes. Added SAME_STEP env handling, truncation bootstrap from final_obs, deterministic rank-0 eval with broadcast, checkpoint resume with RNG state, weighted episode metrics, and regression tests. pytest is green.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed PPO/env/eval audit and fixes. Corrected SAME_STEP autoreset handling, truncation bootstrap from final_obs, non-overlapping reset seeds, rank-0 deterministic evaluation/broadcast, weighted episode metric reduction, checkpoint resume/RNG restore, and config override persistence. Added regression tests. Verification: pytest -q passes (DDP smoke skips when loopback sockets are unavailable in this environment).

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented SARE asymmetric routed experts with route entropy, path coverage, and expert load metrics. Verified one-update smoke run.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented TREG-H with second-hop routing, halting head, transition bias, and ponder penalty. Verified one-update smoke run.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented SRW with unary routed experts, salient-token selection, and gated relational attention. Verified one-update smoke run.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented POR with persistent differentiable option state, termination, duration tracking, and switch-rate metrics. Verified one-update smoke run.

title: Write final greedy-recovery decision memo
close_reason: done: final greedy-recovery decision memo published with evidence-backed stop recommendation

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Published the final no-go narrative in report.md, summary.md, and outputs/reports/greedy_recovery_decision_memo.md, linking the reproduction, checkpoint dynamics, entropy schedule, and self-imitation artifacts. Recommendation: stop routed work for greedy DoorKey claims in this repo unless a new extraction method is justified as a separate project.

title: Run entropy schedule sweep for single_expert and sare
close_reason: done: bounded entropy schedules do not recover greedy DoorKey for single_expert or sare

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Ran source .venv/bin/activate && ./scripts/run_entropy_schedule_sweep.sh, then regenerated outputs/reports/entropy_schedule_report.md and .csv after fixing the launcher to point at the completed output root. Evidence: no tested schedule recovered nonzero greedy DoorKey for single_expert or sare. Best sampled schedules were late_linear:0.01->0.001@0.75 for both variants, but greedy success remained 0.0.

title: Add checkpoint dynamics analysis
close_reason: done: checkpoint dynamics rule out missed transient greedy checkpoints as the main explanation

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Added optional checkpoint archiving (system.archive_checkpoints), checkpoint-series analysis in src/psmn_rl/analysis/checkpoint_dynamics.py, DoorKey checkpoint-scan configs for single_expert and sare, and smoke coverage in tests/test_checkpoint_dynamics_smoke.py. Ran: source .venv/bin/activate && ./scripts/run_checkpoint_dynamics.sh, then regenerated outputs/reports/checkpoint_dynamics_report.md and .csv. Evidence: neither single_expert nor sare ever showed a nonzero greedy checkpoint anywhere in the archived DoorKey series, while sampled success rose to 0.75 and 1.0 respectively.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Epic completed. Final repo conclusion: flat_dense remains the strongest greedy DoorKey control; the smallest successful tokenized recovery is token_dense with ent_coef=0.001; matched SARE still loses under greedy DoorKey evaluation. See report.md, summary.md, outputs/reports/policy_extraction_report.md, outputs/reports/tokenization_gap_report.md, and outputs/experiments/sare_retest/report.md.

title: MiniGrid baseline recovery and routing re-evaluation

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Recovery epic completed. The smoke/falsification lane remains in configs/baseline/ and the original ablation scripts; the canonical recovery lane now lives in configs/diagnostic/ and configs/experiments/. Verification included pytest -q in the repo uv environment plus all-GPU smoke verification of scripts/run_tiny_overfit.sh, scripts/run_minigrid_sanity_suite.sh, scripts/run_minigrid_baseline_suite.sh, and scripts/run_sare_comparison_sweep.sh with PSMN_MAX_UPDATES=1. Durable local reports were generated under outputs/reports/.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed the control-first PPO audit campaign. title: Reproduce accepted DoorKey flat_dense, token_dense, and SARE runs

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Starting exact reproduction of the accepted DoorKey flat_dense, token_dense, and SARE runs in the experiment lane. The baseline test gate passed via source .venv/bin/activate && pytest -q before the reruns.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Current-head DoorKey reproductions match the accepted eval-side conclusion (flat_dense solves; token_dense and SARE remain at zero greedy eval), but train-side metrics drift from the published artifacts. Accepted artifacts record git_commit=59027bb while the reproduction was run on current main; debugging provenance/reproducibility before policy diagnostics.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Saved outputs/reports/reproduction_note.md with exact current-head DoorKey reproduction commands, configs, checkpoints, and metrics. Result: flat_dense still solves greedy DoorKey; token_dense and matched SARE still fail greedy DoorKey. Exact train-side curve reproduction is not possible from the old artifact metadata because run_meta only recorded git_commit and the accepted artifacts point at 59027bb, which cannot uniquely reconstruct the code state. Added git_dirty recording for future runs and verified it with pytest.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Reproduction work completed earlier in this phase. outputs/reports/reproduction_note.md records the exact DoorKey commands, configs, checkpoints, and metrics. The accepted evaluation ranking reproduces on current main, while old train-curve provenance remained incomplete because older run_meta only recorded git_commit without git_dirty.

title: Run tokenization-gap diagnostics and minimal ablations

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Policy-extraction diagnostics are done enough to isolate the next question. DoorKey result on current experiment lane: flat_dense is strong and deterministic under greedy; token_dense remains weak even under sampled eval (best 0.125 success at T=0.5); single_expert and SARE both jump from 0.0 greedy success to 1.0 sampled success at T=1.0. Next step: tokenization-gap diagnostics around fully observed DoorKey, token mixing depth, and representation collapse metrics to find the smallest control-side intervention that materially improves token_dense.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed tokenization-gap diagnostics. Ran baseline, fully observed, depth-4, and depth-4+fully-observed token_dense DoorKey probes under outputs/diagnostics/tokenization_gap and summarized them in outputs/reports/tokenization_gap_report.md. Main result: full observation helps sampled competence, depth alone does not fix partial-observation token_dense, and greedy extraction remains a separate bottleneck.

Existing matched rerun evidence is summarized in outputs/reports/minigrid_sare_report.md, and the new script was smoke-verified on all visible GPUs with PSMN_MAX_UPDATES=1.

title: Conditional fair SARE retest after tokenized recovery

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Recovered token_dense DoorKey control at ent_coef=1e-3: greedy eval_success=0.75, sampled eval_success=1.0, train_return=0.942. Running one fair SARE retest on the same PPO setting alongside flat_dense/token_dense/single_expert at ent_coef=1e-3. No other routed variants in scope.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed fair SARE retest on the recovered token_dense setting (ent_coef=0.001). outputs/experiments/sare_retest/report.md shows flat_dense greedy success 1.0, recovered token_dense greedy success 0.75, single_expert greedy success 0.0, and matched SARE greedy success 0.0. SARE remains negative under the repo's main greedy DoorKey criterion.

title: Add MiniGrid sanity tier and tiny overfit protocol
Optional margin-regularization probe
close_reason: not needed: bounded recovery families already yielded a clean no-go result

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Not run by design. Stop condition reached earlier: checkpoint selection, entropy schedules, and self-imitation all failed to produce nonzero greedy sare on DoorKey. A margin-regularization probe would expand scope after a clean no-go answer.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed baseline-lane reconciliation. Stronger control configs now live under configs/experiments/ for DoorKey, KeyCorridor, and DynamicObstacles without touching configs/baseline/. Canonical script is scripts/run_minigrid_baseline_suite.sh. The recurrent token_gru path is now explicitly documented as diagnostic-only until sequence-aware PPO batching exists. Long-run control evidence is summarized in outputs/reports/minigrid_baseline_report.md; the new script was smoke-verified on all visible GPUs with PSMN_MAX_UPDATES=1.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Launching bounded DoorKey entropy sweep on tokenized controls only. Runs: token_dense ent_coef in {1e-3, 0.0} and single_expert ent_coef in {1e-3, 0.0}. This is directly justified by the policy-extraction result: single_expert already solves under sampled eval but fails under greedy, while token_dense remains soft and underconfident. Goal: recover at least one greedy-competent tokenized control before any routed retest.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed bounded tokenized-control recovery sweep. The smallest successful intervention was lowering token_dense PPO ent_coef from 0.01 to 0.001, which recovered greedy DoorKey success to 0.75 and improved representation health. token_dense ent_coef=0.0 and both single_expert entropy variants did not recover a greedy-competent control.

title: Inspect DoorKey tokenized-control bottlenecks in current code
close_reason: Inspection complete; ranked likely tokenized-control bottlenecks in current DoorKey code with exact model/train/eval/config references and minimal follow-up interventions.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Inspecting current DoorKey control code paths to rank likely bottlenecks in tokenized vs flat_dense performance, with focus on obs encoding, pooling, mixing depth, actor/value inputs, and greedy eval behavior.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Findings: strongest shared bottlenecks appear to be token-level mean pooling of all MiniGrid cells plus a lightly weighted direction token, actor/value heads consuming only pooled features, and no cross-token mixing at all in single_expert/SARE beyond pooling. Train always samples while eval defaults to greedy argmax, so sampled eval should be checked before concluding policy learning is absent.

title: Reproduce current DoorKey greedy-recovery baseline
close_reason: done: current DoorKey greedy-recovery baseline reproduced cleanly on current main

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Reproduced the current DoorKey calibration baseline with: source .venv/bin/activate && PSMN_OUTPUT_ROOT=outputs/reproductions/greedy_recovery_baseline PSMN_EVAL_EPISODES=32 ./scripts/run_sare_fair_retest.sh. Output artifacts: outputs/reproductions/greedy_recovery_baseline/report.md and outputs/reports/greedy_recovery_reproduction_note.md. Reproduced ranking matches published calibration artifacts: flat_dense greedy success 1.0, token_dense_ent1e3 greedy success 0.75, single_expert_ent1e3 greedy success 0.0 with sampled success 0.75, sare_ent1e3 greedy success 0.0 with sampled success 1.0.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Fresh DoorKey DDP reruns are currently blocked by a runtime issue outside the trainer: nvidia-smi still lists 4 GPUs, but fresh PyTorch CUDA init now fails with cudaGetDeviceCount error 304 and torchrun standalone falls into a bad localhost:0 rendezvous path before model code starts. Existing accepted artifact roots and previously reproduced outputs remain readable. Captured evidence with: nvidia-smi -L; python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available(), torch.cuda.device_count())'; repeated torchrun retries via scripts/run_sare_fair_retest.sh.

Added rollout/eval action entropy, max-prob, logit-margin, greedy-match logging; added temperature-scaled eval; ran outputs/diagnostics/policy_extraction/doorkey and saved outputs/reports/policy_extraction_report.md. Main result: flat_dense is sharp under greedy; token_dense is weak even under sampled eval; single_expert and SARE are strong under sampled eval but fail greedy.
